steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent - Create your own agent by declaring instructions..." option in the "New Project" pop-up menu within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Add an Action" option in the dropdown menu of the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Start with an MCP server Preview" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click inside the "Enter your MCP server URL" input field in the MCP Server URL prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Type 'https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/' into the "Enter your MCP server URL" input field in the MCP Server URL prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press Enter to submit the MCP server URL ("https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/") in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" dialog of Visual Studio Code to select the project root directory.
Click the "Start" link in the MCP server configuration instructions within the "mcp.json" file in Visual Studio Code, next to the "ATK: Fetch action from MCP" text.
Click the "Allow" button in the authentication prompt titled "The MCP Server Definition 'apigithubc' wants to authenticate to GitHub" at the top of the VS Code window.
Click inside the "Username or email address" input field on the GitHub login page for Visual Studio Code authentication.
Type  ${{env:GITHUB_ACCOUNT_NAME}}  into the 'Username or email address' input field on the GitHub login page for Visual Studio Code authorization.
Click inside the "Password" input field on the GitHub login page for Visual Studio Code authorization.
Enter ${{secret:GITHUB_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the Password input field on the GitHub sign-in page for Visual Studio Code authorization.
Click the green "Sign in" button on the GitHub login page for Visual Studio Code, below the password input field.
Click inside the six-digit code input field on the GitHub Two-factor authentication page to focus and enable code entry.
Enter ${{mfa_code:GITHUB_MFA_SECRET}} into the two-factor authentication code input field on the GitHub two-factor authentication web page.
Click the green "Continue" button to authorize Visual Studio Code for the signed-in GitHub account "ATKTest" on the GitHub OAuth consent page.
Click the "Open Visual Studio Code - URL Handler" button in the browser popup prompt to allow vscode.dev to open Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Close" (X) button in the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to exit the application.
Click the "Close" (X) button in the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window displaying the "GitHub Authentication – Launching Visual Studio Code" page.
Click on the "Fetch from MCP" link in the code comment section of the mcp.json file within Visual Studio Code's editor interface.
Click the "ai-plugin.js" file entry in the manifest selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Type 'get' into the search input field of the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the search input field in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog within Visual Studio Code to focus and enter text.
Click the blue plus (+) button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot can interact with" dialog in Visual Studio Code to add the selected operation.
Click the search input field in the "Select Authentication Type" dropdown dialog of Visual Studio Code to activate it for text entry.
Type 'entra' into the "Select Authentication Type" input field in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Entra SSO" option in the "Select Authentication Type" dropdown within Visual Studio Code, after typing "entra" in the filter box.
Click inside the "ai-plugin.json" code editor in Visual Studio Code at line 16, column 14, placing the cursor within the "parameters" object of the JSON schema.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while editing the "ai-plugin.json" file in the "vscluse_app_CrcvP" workspace.
Type 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette search bar to filter Microsoft 365 Agents commands.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" option in the command palette dropdown within Visual Studio Code to initiate the provisioning process.
Click the input field in the "Enter Microsoft Entra SSO client id for registration in OpenAPI Description Document" prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.

Type ${{env:DA_MCP_ENTRA_SSO_CLIENTID}}  into the "Enter Microsoft Entra SSO client id for registration in OpenAPI Description Document" input field in the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents extension modal.
Press the Enter key to confirm the client ID input in the "Enter Microsoft Entra SSO client id for registration in OpenAPI Description Document" prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:'Notifications: Show Notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the window.
Press Enter to select \"Notifications: Show Notifications\" from the VS Code command palette.
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/build/manifest.dev.json exist. Timeout: 120s
@assertion notification \"provision stage executed successfully\" exist.
